raw_text:
HAEMATOLOGY CLINIC NOTE - Date: 2026-01-22
Facility: Lagos University Teaching Hospital, Lagos, Nigeria

Patient: 22-year-old Nigerian female presenting for routine sickle cell disease follow-up.

HPI: Patient has known sickle cell disease (HbSS genotype) diagnosed at age 2 via haemoglobin electrophoresis. Over the past 12 months, she has experienced 6 vaso-occlusive crises (VOCs) requiring hospitalization, with the most severe episode 2 months ago involving acute chest syndrome requiring oxygen supplementation and exchange transfusion. She was started on hydroxyurea 500mg daily 8 months ago, subsequently titrated to 1000mg daily, but continues to have frequent crises. She reports chronic joint pain in bilateral hips and knees rated 4/10 at baseline, managed with paracetamol. She denies fever, jaundice worsening, or priapism. She is a university student studying pharmacy.

Vitals: BP 108/68 mmHg, HR 92 bpm, Temp 37.0C, RR 18, SpO2 96% RA. Weight 52 kg, Height 165 cm, BMI 19.1.

Physical Exam: Thin-appearing young woman. Mild scleral icterus. Pale conjunctivae. No lymphadenopathy. Heart: systolic flow murmur grade II/VI. Lungs clear. Abdomen: spleen not palpable (auto-splenectomy suspected). Mild tenderness bilateral hip joints without effusion. Chronic leg ulcer right medial malleolus (2 cm, granulating).

Labs: Hgb 7.8 g/dL (baseline 7.5-8.5), HbF 12% (target >15%), HbS 78%, WBC 11.2, Plt 380, reticulocyte count 8.5%, total bilirubin 3.2 mg/dL (indirect predominant), LDH 420 U/L, ferritin 280 ng/mL, creatinine 0.7 mg/dL.

Assessment and Plan:
1. Sickle cell disease (HbSS) with frequent VOCs despite hydroxyurea. Increase hydroxyurea to 1500mg daily (target 20-25 mg/kg). Monitor CBC in 4 weeks for myelosuppression.
2. If VOC frequency does not improve, consider crizanlizumab 5mg/kg IV as add-on therapy or evaluate for chronic transfusion programme.
3. Right leg ulcer - continue wound care, zinc supplementation. Wound clinic referral.
4. Ensure up-to-date vaccinations: pneumococcal, meningococcal, influenza, hepatitis B.
5. Folic acid 5mg daily ongoing. Penicillin V 250mg BID for infection prophylaxis.
6. Transcranial Doppler: not indicated at this age. Echocardiogram to screen for pulmonary hypertension.
7. Follow-up in 6 weeks with CBC and HbF level.
medical_history: Acute chest syndrome 2 months ago requiring exchange transfusion
